steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: f(x) = ab² + c contains no x — a, b, and c are constants, so ab² + c is a fixed number. The graph is a horizontal line. The question asks how many x-values satisfy f(x) = 0, i.e., how many times the horizontal line crosses the x-axis.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Three': A horizontal line can cross the x-axis at most once (if it lies on it). ✗
  • Choice B — 'Two': Same reason — a horizontal line can't cross the x-axis twice. ✗
  • Choice C — 'One': Only if the line lies exactly on the x-axis (ab² + c = 0). The graph shows the line does not lie on the x-axis. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Zero': The horizontal line is above or below the x-axis but never touches it, so f(x) = 0 has no solution. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
